text: In what year did the United States declare independence?
explanation: The Declaration of Independence was adopted on July 4, 1776.

text: Who was the first President of the United States?
explanation: George Washington served as the first President from 1789 to 1797.

text: The Titanic sank on its maiden voyage.
explanation: The Titanic sank on April 15, 1912, during its first voyage from Southampton to New York.

text: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza?
explanation: The Ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramids around 2560 BCE.

text: What year did World War I begin?
explanation: World War I began on July 28, 1914, following the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand.

text: Who was the British Prime Minister during most of World War II?
explanation: Winston Churchill served as Prime Minister from 1940 to 1945, leading Britain through WWII.

text: The Roman Empire fell in 476 AD.
explanation: The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 AD when Romulus Augustulus was deposed.

text: Which explorer is credited with discovering America in 1492?
explanation: Christopher Columbus made his first voyage to the Americas in 1492.

text: What was the name of the ship that brought the Pilgrims to America in 1620?
explanation: The Mayflower transported 102 passengers to Plymouth, Massachusetts.

text: Who was the last Queen of France before the French Revolution?
explanation: Marie Antoinette was executed during the French Revolution in 1793.

text: What year did the Civil War end?
explanation: The Civil War ended on April 9, 1865, when General Lee surrendered at Appomattox.

text: Pearl Harbor was attacked on December 7, 1941.
explanation: Japan attacked Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941, leading the US to enter WWII.

text: Who was the first man to walk on the moon?
explanation: Neil Armstrong took the first steps on the moon on July 20, 1969.

text: Which President issued the Emancipation Proclamation?
explanation: Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation on January 1, 1863.

text: The Cold War ended with the fall of the Soviet Union in 1991.
explanation: The Soviet Union dissolved on December 26, 1991, marking the end of the Cold War.
